acquisition.value: On 2025-08-29, Ivybrook OpCo LLC (DE) — wholly owned by Ivybrook HoldCo, LLC (DE), in turn wholly owned by Crux I Ivybrook (Aggregator), LP (DE) — acquired all membership interests in Ivybrook Franchising, LLC, McWilliams Education Services, LLC, and Catapult Industries, LLC.

founder_retained_interest.value: Drew and Jennifer McWilliams hold interest in HoldCo via McWilliams IvyBrook Holdings, LLC (formed 2025-08-21, NC).

parent_address.value: Crux: 3838 Oak Lawn Ave # 1500, Dallas, TX 75219

renewal_fee.value: greater of $10,000 or 10% of then-current initial franchise fee

liquidated_damages_formula.value: trailing-12-month avg monthly Royalty × months remaining in term (payable on termination for cause)

continuing_operation_fee_formula.value: greater of $1,000 or 150% of weekly royalty per week, cap = then-current initial franchise fee

affiliate_supplier_student_hub.value: Catapult Industries, LLC — owner and exclusive supplier of The Student Hub (school mgmt + payment processing software)

franchisor_data_access_right.value: Franchisor has independent right to access all franchisee technology data at any time without notice (FA 12.6, 12.7).

territory_definition.value: Lesser of 125,000 population or 3-mile radius around the location

exclusivity_scope.value: Protected only against another Ivybrook Academy School under the Marks; franchisor retains customer-solicitation, alternative-channel, merchandise, and non-Ivybrook-brand rights inside the Territory

online_sales_rights.value: Franchisor retains all online sale rights; franchisee may not sell via mail order, phone, mobile app, internet, or any off-site channel except approved off-site events

noncompete_post_term_geo.value: Greater of the Territory or 10-mile radius of franchise location or any other Franchised Business (subject to state law)

item19_fpr.population_criteria: Franchisee schools open 2+ years on or before Jan 1, 2023 (Table 2B, n=24); open 3+ years on or before Jan 1, 2022 (Table 2C, n=18); classroom-count segmentation of 3+ year franchisees (Table 3, n=19); affiliate-owned Weddington NC school (Table 2A, n=1).

table_2a_affiliate.note: Imputed royalty and Brand Fund; affiliate does not actually pay these.

table_3_by_classroom.contradiction: Table 3 n=19 vs Table 2C n=18 → one-unit discrepancy flagged.

critical_caveats: Affiliate Weddington school is an extreme outlier: 0/24 franchisees matched its Gross Revenue, 2/24 matched its EBITDA.
Brand Fund contribution for affiliate is imputed only — affiliate does not actually pay royalty/BF, inflating comparability.
Table 2B/2C exclude schools not open at least two full years, non-compliant with brand standards, or that ceased operations in 2024.
Labor excludes owner salaries; EBITDA is not net income.

Population 40 total schools as of Dec 31, 2024 — but Item 20 reports 41 total outlets end of 2024 (40 franchised + 1 company). Item 19 says '40 total schools were in operation as of December 31, 2024.' 1-unit mismatch.

auditor_name.note: Exhibit J text layer empty in this run. Prior run (ivybrook-academy-2025) identifies Reese CPA — see B1 benchmark. Not re-confirmed here without OCR.

stockholders_equity_2024_usd.note: Promoted in A2 from Virginia addendum risk factor. Investment range exceeds equity.

auditor_name.value: Reese CPA LLC, Ft. Collins, CO
auditor_name.provenance: B4 backfill from runs/ivybrook-academy-2025 (prior run vision-OCR recovery)

opinion_type.value: Unmodified (clean). Going concern explicitly evaluated; no modification issued.

going_concern_language.value: No going concern modification. Auditor explicitly evaluated and concluded entity can continue.

deferred_franchise_fees.note: Deferred franchise fees = 97% of total liabilities; dominant balance-sheet item explaining the low stockholder's equity despite positive net income.

stockholders_equity.note: Originally promoted from VA addendum; consistent with $3,894,365 − $3,548,503 = $345,862.

financial_health_summary.equity_consistency_check: PASS — VA addendum equity ($345,862) exactly matches total_assets − total_liabilities from prior run benchmark.
financial_health_summary.interpretation: Franchisor is profitable (net income $313k on $2.17M revenue) but the balance sheet is dominated by deferred franchise fee liabilities ($3.4M from signed-but-unopened units), producing a thin stockholder's equity ($346k). Auditor did not modify the opinion or issue going-concern language. Illinois and VA regulators independently flagged the equity-vs-investment gap as a disclosure concern.

fee_deferral.rule: Initial franchise + development fees deferred until franchisor has satisfied pre-opening obligations AND franchisee has commenced operations; imposed by Illinois AG due to franchisor's financial condition

forum_selection.rule: Out-of-state litigation clauses void per IL FDA §4 (mediation/arbitration may be out of state)

anti_waiver.rule: IL FDA §41 voids any waiver of IL FDA compliance; no acknowledgment can waive state franchise law claims or disclaim reliance

forum_selection.rule: Minn. Stat. §80C.21 / Rule 2860.4400J — litigation cannot be required outside MN (arbitration may be)

termination_notice_and_cure.rule: Minn. Stat. §80C.14 subds. 3-5 — 90 days notice, 60-day cure; 180 days non-renewal notice

general_release.rule: No release of MN Franchises Act claims as a condition to renewal or assignment

anti_waiver.rule: No waiver of §§80C.01–80C.22 liability; no acknowledgment can waive claims or disclaim reliance

trademark_protection.rule: Franchisor will protect franchisee mark usage and indemnify infringement claims while franchisee in compliance

termination_standard.rule: VA §13.1-564 — cancellation only for reasonable cause; defaults not constituting reasonable cause may be unenforceable

undue_influence.rule: VA §13.1-564 — undue influence inducing rights surrender is unenforceable

integration_merger.rule: Item 17(t) amended under VA law; outside representations may not be enforceable

anti_waiver.rule: No acknowledgment can waive state franchise law claims or disclaim reliance

financial_condition_risk_factor.rule: Estimated initial investment exceeds franchisor stockholder's equity as of 12/31/2024 ($345,862)

unresolveds_consolidated.description: Exhibit J (pp 168–197) image-only; auditor/opinion/full financials not readable. Partially mitigated by VA addendum equity disclosure.
Exhibit K label missing; TOC vs body contradiction.
Item 19 '40 total schools' vs Item 20 '41 total outlets' mismatch.
Customer-evaluation minimum score termination trigger with undisclosed threshold.
Unilateral franchisor tech data access (FA 12.6/12.7) without notice.
Brand Fund planned increase 1% → 2% in 2026 without specified mechanism.

contradictions_consolidated.description: Exhibit K label vs body label L
Cover Special Risk #2 financial condition vs unreadable Exhibit J
contradictions_consolidated.resolution_status: partially resolved via VA addendum equity disclosure

special_risks_disclosed_on_cover: Out-of-State Dispute Resolution (mediation/arbitration in North Carolina)
Financial Condition (franchisor's financial condition, see Item 21, calls into question its ability to provide services and support)
Supplier Control (must purchase all or nearly all supplies from franchisor/affiliates/designees at prices set by them)
Unopened Franchises (franchisor has signed a significant number of unopened agreements)

unresolveds.description: Exhibit J (audited financial statements, pp 168–197) is image-only; auditor name, opinion type, balance sheet, income statement, cash flow, and any going-concern language are not directly readable without vision OCR. Franchisor's own cover-page Special Risk #2 flags the financial condition — the specific concern cannot be confirmed from text alone.

Exhibit K label missing. TOC labels K=Current Franchisees, L=Franchisee Disclosure Questionnaire; body labels the franchisee list as Exhibit L and includes no Exhibit K. Questionnaire may be absent or mislabeled.
Table 3 (19 franchisees by classroom count) vs Table 2C (18 franchisees open 3+ years) 1-unit population mismatch.

Item 19 states '40 total schools were in operation as of December 31, 2024.' Item 20 reports 41 total outlets end 2024 (40 franchised + 1 company). 1-unit mismatch likely from inclusion/exclusion of the affiliate-owned school.
Customer-evaluation minimum score threshold is listed as a non-curable termination trigger (Item 17) but the threshold itself is not disclosed in Item 11 or Item 17 narrative.
Unilateral technology data-access right in FA 12.6/12.7 allows franchisor to access any franchisee technology at any time without notice.
Brand Fund planned to rise from 1% to 2% of Gross Revenues in 2026 per Item 6; timing and mechanism of the change not specified.
